revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]][[古墳#埋葬施設]]へのリダイレクト
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[古墳#埋葬施設]]

'''石室'''（せきしつ）とは、古墳の墳丘の中に造られた石造りの埋葬施設である。棺を直接覆う'''槨'''とは区別される。
== 概要 == 大きく分けると上部が開いた[[竪穴式石室]]と横から出入りできる[[横穴式石室]]に分けられる。
古墳時代初期には竪穴式石室は一回限りの埋葬を目的とし、棺の周りに石を積み上げ、天井石を載せ、埋めたものである。
初期末前後に北九州で成立した[[竪穴系横口式石室]]と呼ばれる横口式石室の初期のものは、竪穴式石室の短辺の一方に入口と通路を設け、出入りが可能で追葬できるようにしてある。
後期・終末期には石棺式石室や横口式石棺と呼ぶ石棺・石棺状施設に出入り口を設けた埋葬施設が出現する。
構築方法は、割石小口積み・切石積みなどがある。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 参考文献 == *杉山晋作「石室」/江坂輝彌・芹沢長介・坂詰秀一編『新日本考古学小辞典』ニュー・サイエンス社 2005年 ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 *白石太一郎「石室」/田中琢・佐原真編『日本考古学事典』三省堂 2003年 ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 *永原慶二監修 石上英一他編集『岩波 日本史辞典』岩波書店 1999年 ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8
== 関連項目 == *[[古墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:せつかん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:日本の考古遺物]] [[Category:縄文時代]] [[Category:弥生時代]] [[Category:古墳時代]]

[[ファイル:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|石室入口（[[牟佐大塚古墳]]）]] '''石室'''（せきしつ）とは、古墳の墳丘の中に造られた石造りの埋葬施設である。棺を直接覆う'''槨'''とは区別される。
== 概要 == 大きく分けると上部が開いた[[竪穴式石室]]と横から出入りできる[[横穴式石室]]に分けられる。
古墳時代初期には竪穴式石室は一回限りの埋葬を目的とし、棺の周りに石を積み上げ、天井石を載せ、埋めたものである。
初期末前後に北九州で成立した[[竪穴系横口式石室]]と呼ばれる横口式石室の初期のものは、竪穴式石室の短辺の一方に入口と通路を設け、出入りが可能で追葬できるようにしてある。
後期・終末期には石棺式石室や横口式石棺と呼ぶ石棺・石棺状施設に出入り口を設けた埋葬施設が出現する。
構築方法は、割石小口積み・切石積みなどがある。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 参考文献 == *杉山晋作「石室」/江坂輝彌・芹沢長介・坂詰秀一編『新日本考古学小辞典』ニュー・サイエンス社 2005年 ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 *白石太一郎「石室」/田中琢・佐原真編『日本考古学事典』三省堂 2003年 ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 *永原慶二監修 石上英一他編集『岩波 日本史辞典』岩波書店 1999年 ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8

revision.text:
{{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Otsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, capped with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Artifacts]] [[Category:Jōmon period]] [[Category:Yayoi period]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Stone structures]] [[ja:石室]]

The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, capped with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Otsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, capped with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, capped with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} a: [[ja:石室]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chamber]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chambers]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Stone chambers-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chambers]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chamber]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chambers]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Stone chambers-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chambers]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chamber]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Stone chambers]][[Category:kk]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]]

{{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, capped with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, capped with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, capped with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, capped with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} a: [[ja:石室]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chamber]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chambers]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Stone Chamber]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chambers]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chamber]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chambers]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Stone Chamber]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chambers]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chamber]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Stone chambers]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[sn:Stone chambers]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Stone chambers]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Stone chambers]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Stone chambers]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Stone chambers]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Stone chambers]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Stone chambers]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Stone chambers]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:石室]][[en:Stone Chamber]][[Category:石室]] [[Category:石室]][[en:Stone Chamber]][[Category:石室]] [[Category:横口式石槨]][[sn:Stone Chamber]][[Category:横口式石槨]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Stone Chamber]][[Category:qq]]

{{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, caqqed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.

{{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.

{{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} a: [[ja:石室]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Stone chamber]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]] By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|12=draft}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=draft|12=draft}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} a: {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Stone chamber]] [[Category:Stone chambers]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Stone Chamber]] [[ja:石室]] [[sn:Stone chambers]] [[en:Stone Chamber]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Stone Chamber]][[Category:qq]]

By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} a: {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Stone chamber]] [[Category:Stone chambers]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Stone Chamber]] [[ja:石室]] [[sn:Stone chambers]] [[en:Stone Chamber]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Stone Chamber]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:石室 up to rev 90176755
revision.text:
{{脚注の不足|date=2016年3月}} [[ファイル:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|石室入口（[[牟佐大塚古墳]]）]] '''石室'''（せきしつ）とは、[[古墳]]の[[墳丘墓|墳丘]]の中に造られた石造りの埋葬施設である。棺を直接覆う'''槨'''とは区別される。 == 概要 == 上部が開いた[[竪穴式石室]]と、横から出入りできる[[横穴式石室]]に大別される。 [[古墳時代]]初期に見られるものとしては、竪穴式石室は一回限りの埋葬を目的としたもので、棺の周りに石を積み上げ、天井石を載せて埋めたものである。
初期末前後に北九州で成立した竪穴系横口式石室と呼ばれる横口式石室の初期のものは、竪穴式石室の短辺の一方に入口と通路を設け、出入りが可能で追葬できるようにしてある。
後期・終末期には[[石棺式石室]]や[[横口式石槨]]と呼ぶ石棺・石棺状施設に出入り口を設けた埋葬施設が出現する。
構築方法は、割石小口積み・切石積みなどがある。 == 参考文献 == *杉山晋作「石室」/江坂輝彌・[[芹沢長介]]・[[坂詰秀一]]編『新日本考古学小辞典』[[ニュー・サイエンス社]] 2005年 ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 *[[白石太一郎]]「石室」/[[田中琢]]・[[佐原真]]編『日本考古学事典』[[三省堂]]　2003年　ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 *[[永原慶二]]監修　[[石上英一]]他編集『岩波　日本史辞典』[[岩波書店]]　1999年　ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == 関連項目 == *[[古墳]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:せつかん}} [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:遺物]] [[Category:縄文時代]] [[Category:弥生時代]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:石造建築物]]

During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11585502]] Wikidata name: stone chamber [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichiro Shiraishi|qq=}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|qq=}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] a: {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石室|version=90176755|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Stone Chamber]] [[ja:石室]] [[sn:Stone chambers]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Yayoi period]] [[Category:Stone buildings]] [[Category:Jōmon period]] [[Category:Archaeological artifacts]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11585502]] Wikidata name: stone chamber [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichiro Shiraishi|qq=|1=Taichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580156}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|qq=|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] a: {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石室|version=90176755|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Stone Chamber]] [[ja:石室]] [[sn:Stone chambers]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Yayoi period]] [[Category:Stone buildings]] [[Category:Jōmon period]] [[Category:Archaeological artifacts]]

During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.

[[ja:石室]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11585502]] Wikidata name: stone chamber [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichiro Shiraishi|1=Taichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580156}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] a: {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石室|version=90176755|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:石室]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Yayoi period]] [[Category:Stone buildings]] [[Category:Jōmon period]] [[Category:Archaeological artifacts]] {{wikidata link|Q11585502}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:石室]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11585502]] Wikidata name: stone chamber [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichiro Shiraishi|1=Taichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580156}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] a: {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石室|version=90176755|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:石室]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Yayoi period]] [[Category:Stone buildings]] [[Category:Jōmon period]] [[Category:Archaeological artifacts]] {{wikidata link|Q11585502}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11585502}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichiro Shiraishi|1=Taichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580156}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. [[ja:石室]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11585502]] Wikidata name: stone chamber [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichiro Shiraishi|1=Taichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580156}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] a: {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石室|version=90176755|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:石室]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Yayoi period]] [[Category:Stone buildings]] [[Category:Jōmon period]] [[Category:Archaeological artifacts]] {{wikidata link|Q11585502}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11585502}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[ja:石室]] [[ja:石室]] [[ja:石室]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11585502]] Wikidata name: stone chamber [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichiro Shiraishi|1=Taichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580156}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] a: {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石室|version=90176755|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:石室]] {{wikidata link|Q11585502}} [[Category:Archaeological artifacts]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Jōmon period]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Stone buildings]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yayoi period]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichiro Shiraishi|1=Taichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580156}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] a: {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石室|version=90176755|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石室]] {{wikidata link|Q11585502}} [[Category:Archaeological artifacts]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Jōmon period]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Stone buildings]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yayoi period]]

[[ja:石室]] [[ja:石室]] [[ja:石室]] {{Refimprove|date=March 2016}} [[File:Musa Otsuka Kofun 05.jpg|thumb|Entrance of the stone chamber ([[Musa Ōtsuka Kofun]])]] A '''stone chamber''' (石室, sekishitsu) refers to a stone-built burial facility constructed within the {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} mound. It is distinct from a '''coffin chamber''' (槨), such as {{ill|yokoguchi-style stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoguchi-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} which directly encloses the coffin. == Overview == Stone chambers are broadly categorized into {{ill|pit-style stone chambers|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} and {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} based on their structure. The pit-style stone chambers, found in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, were designed for single-use burials. Stones were stacked around the coffin, ca11ed with a ceiling stone, and then buried beneath the mound.
By the end of the early Kofun period, the corridor-style stone chamber evolved in northern Kyushu. These chambers, referred to as pit-based horizontal entrance chambers, featured an entrance and passage on one side of the short axis of the pit, allowing for reuse and additional burials.
During the late Kofun period and its terminal phase, burial facilities such as {{ill|stone-coffin-style stone chambers|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=式石室|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone-coffin-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} and {{ill|horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal entrance stone sarcophagi|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} emerged. These chambers incorporated entrances into coffin-like structures.
Construction methods varied, including techniques like split stone small face stacking and dressed stone stacking. == References == * Shinsaku Sugiyama, "Stone Chambers," in *New Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Teruya Esaka, Chosuke Serizawa, and Shuichi Sakazume, New Science Co., 2005. ISBN 4-8216-0511-2 * {{ill|Taichiro Shiraishi|ja|白石太一郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichiro Shiraishi|1=Taichirō Shiraishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580156}}, "Stone Chambers," in *Japanese Archaeology Dictionary*, edited by Taku Tanaka and Makoto Sahara, Sanseido, 2003. ISBN 978-4-385-15835-8 * {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, edited by Eiichi Ishigami et al., *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 978-4-00-080093-8 == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} a: {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|石室|version=90176755|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:石室]] {{wikidata link|Q11585502}} [[Category:Archaeological artifacts]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Jōmon period]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Stone buildings]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yayoi period]] [[Category:pages]]
